explanation.rule: The suffix -ation (and its variants -tion, -ion) is added to verbs to form nouns describing a process, state, or result. It is extremely common in academic and formal English.

groups.note: Added to verbs to form nouns. The base verb sometimes changes spelling (e.g. educate → education, prepare → preparation).

examples.definition: the process of changing to suit a new situation
a feeling of great respect and approval
a reason or desire that causes someone to act
the exchange of information or ideas
the process of getting ready for something

wordForms.definition: the process of changing to suit a new situation
wordForms.example: The film is a brilliant adaptation of the original novel.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to change in order to deal with new conditions

wordForms.definition: a feeling of great respect and approval
wordForms.example: She gazed at the painting with deep admiration.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to respect and approve of someone or something

wordForms.definition: a reason or desire that causes someone to act
wordForms.example: His main motivation for studying abroad was to improve his language skills.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to give someone a reason or desire to do something

wordForms.definition: the exchange of information, news, or ideas
wordForms.example: Good communication is essential in any successful team.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to share or exchange information with others

wordForms.definition: the act of arranging things systematically, or a structured group
wordForms.example: The organisation of the conference took several months.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to arrange or plan something in a systematic way

wordForms.definition: the process of teaching and learning
wordForms.example: A good education opens many doors in life.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to teach someone over a period of time

wordForms.definition: a formal test of knowledge or skill
wordForms.example: She studied hard before the final examination.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to look at or test something carefully

wordForms.definition: the ability to form new ideas or mental images
wordForms.rootDefinition: to form a picture or idea in your mind

wordForms.definition: the ability to focus attention on one thing
wordForms.example: Loud music affects her concentration while studying.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to give all your attention to something

wordForms.definition: the process of getting ready for something
wordForms.example: Preparation is key to a successful presentation.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to make something or someone ready

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: The film is a brilliant ___ of the bestselling novel.
Good ___ skills are vital in any professional environment.
Her ___ for volunteering was a genuine desire to help others.
She passed the ___ with the highest mark in her class.
Thorough ___ before an interview increases your chances of success.
The ___ of the annual festival requires careful planning.

transformExercises.sentence: The ___ of the novel into a film took over two years. (ADAPT)
He expressed great ___ for his mentor's dedication. (ADMIRE)
Creative ___ is a skill that employers look for in candidates. (IMAGINE)
Loud noise in the library disturbs students' ___. (CONCENTRATE)
